gāyantiyā.meaning: of (a woman) singing; while she sings
gāyantiyā.gram: present participle of gāyati (√gā), feminine oblique singular
breakdown: Present participle, feminine oblique singular ending.
Past participle suffix: what is sung.

halaṁ.gram: indeclinable particle (h- prefixed variant of alaṁ)

halaṁ/alaṁ.gram: indeclinable particle (halaṁ is a variant of alaṁ)

harate.gram: verb, 3rd person singular present middle voice of harati (√har)
breakdown: Present stem of harati, to carry.

haraṇanti.gram: neuter action noun, nominative/accusative singular + quotation particle ti

breakdown: Root associated with the yellow-green colour.

harāyati.gram: verb, 3rd person singular present, denominative from hiri (shame)

breakdown: Stem of harāyati, to be ashamed.
Playing, amusements (past participle of kīḷati), neuter plural.

hatapahate.meaning: injured and killed; struck down and slain
hatapahate.gram: masculine compound (past participles), locative singular or accusative plural
breakdown: Injured, struck, tortured (past participle of hanati).
Killed, struck down (past participle of paharati).

hatthigavāssavaḷavāpaṭiggahaṇā.meaning: (abstaining) from accepting elephants, cattle, horses and mares
hatthigavāssavaḷavāpaṭiggahaṇā.gram: neuter compound action noun, ablative singular

hehiti.gram: verb, 3rd person singular future of hoti (√hū)
breakdown: Future stem of hoti, to be.

hessati.gram: verb, 3rd person singular future of hoti (√hū)
breakdown: Future stem of hoti, to be.

hettha.meaning: for here; because in this matter
breakdown: Here, herein. Sandhi: hi + ettha > hettha.

hetuppabhavā.meaning: arising from a cause; having their origin in a cause
hetuppabhavā.gram: compound adjective, plural or ablative singular
breakdown: Arising, origination (pa + √bhū; the doubled pp is sandhi doubling).
hetuppabhavā.goenka: The phrase echoes the famous verse by which Sāriputta gained the eye of Dhamma: ye dhammā hetuppabhavā — "whatever phenomena arise from a cause, of these the Tathāgata has told the cause, and also their cessation."

hetusambhārapaccayā.meaning: by means of cause, constituents and condition
hetusambhārapaccayā.gram: compound noun, ablative singular or plural

breakdown: Thus, so. Sandhi: hi + evaṁ > hevaṁ.

hirīnisedho.meaning: restrained by shame; one whom conscience holds back

hirīsuttaṁ.meaning: the Hirī Sutta, the Discourse on Shame (moral conscience)

hissa.meaning: because of this; for of this (with reference to kissa)
hissa.gram: particle hi + genitive pronoun, fused in verse
breakdown: Of this, of him (genitive). Sandhi: hi + assa > hissa.

hitajjhāsayataṁ.meaning: the state of intending others' welfare; the disposition wishing for benefit
breakdown: Intention, wish, disposition (adhi + āsaya; the doubled jjh is sandhi).
Abstract suffix -tā in the accusative: the state of being so.

hitakāmā.gram: compound adjective, plural or feminine form

hitesinā.meaning: by one wishing welfare; by a well-wisher
breakdown: Seeking, wishing (esī, from √es, to seek), instrumental singular.

hontu.meaning: may they be; let them be
hontu.gram: verb, 3rd person plural imperative of hoti (√hū)
breakdown: Present stem of hoti, to be.
hontu.goenka: Hontu appears in the mettā aspiration chanted in the tradition: sabbe sattā sukhī hontu — may all beings be happy.

hutaṁ.meaning: what has been sacrificed; an oblation
hutaṁ.gram: neuter noun (past participle of juhati, √hu), nominative/accusative singular

hāpeti.gram: verb, 3rd person singular present, causative of jahati (√hā)
breakdown: Causative stem of √hā, to abandon: to cause to be left, neglect.

hāraye.meaning: would cause to take; should have (something) carried
hāraye.gram: verb, 3rd person singular optative, causative of harati (√har)
breakdown: Causative stem of harati, to carry: to cause to take.

hīnappaṇītatāyāti.meaning: "...as inferiority and superiority" (with closing quotation marker)
hīnappaṇītatāyāti.gram: feminine abstract compound noun, oblique singular + quotation particle ti
breakdown: High, exalted, excellent (the doubled pp is sandhi doubling).
Abstract suffix -tā in oblique case: the state of being low and excellent.

hīnavīriyo.meaning: of low energy; lacking in effort

hīno.summary: Appears in kāmasukhallikānuyogo, the low pursuit of sensual pleasure, one of the two extremes rejected in the first discourse.
hīno.gram: past participle of hāyati (√hā) used as adjective, masculine nominative singular
breakdown: Past participle suffix: left behind, hence low, inferior.

hīnādhimuttikehi.meaning: with those inclined to the low; by people of inferior disposition
breakdown: Intent upon, inclined to (adhi + √muc). Sandhi: hīna + adhi- > hīnādhi-.

hīnāyāvattissāmī.meaning: "I will return to the low (life)" — revert to lay life
hīnāyāvattissāmī.gram: verb phrase fused by sandhi; dative adjective + 1st person singular future
breakdown: To the low (state), the inferior lay life (dative of hīna).
I will return, turn back (future of āvattati). Sandhi lengthening at the junction.
